extracted_information: ShareJS is a server and client library designed for concurrent editing of content using Operational Transformation (OT). It runs on NodeJS for the server and can be used in NodeJS or web browsers for the client. ShareJS supports operational transformation on plain-text and arbitrary JSON data. It is important to note that **ShareJS has transitioned to ShareDB** (github.com/share/sharedb), which is its successor. ShareJS relies on LiveDB for its database backend and data model. It offers a flexible, transport-agnostic communication model between client and server, requiring in-order message delivery and a WebSocket-like API.

specifications.name: ShareJS
specifications.browser_support: Should work with all browsers down to IE5.5 (regularly tested with FF, Safari, Chrome, occasionally IE8+)
transport_requirements: Node object stream to the server

features.name: Server API
features.description: Provides methods to create a ShareJS server instance, handle client communication streams, expose a REST API, and implement access control via middleware.

features.name: Client API
features.description: Enables creation of connections and document references, management of document state (subscribe, fetch, create, delete), and submission of operations. Supports debugging features.

features.name: Document Editing Contexts
features.description: Thin wrappers around submitOp that prevent contexts from receiving their own operations and mix in API methods specific to the OT type, simplifying editing.

features.name: Document Events
features.description: Documents are event emitters, notifying about changes in state and operations.

features.name: Collaborative Editing
features.description: Enables concurrent editing of text and JSON data across multiple clients, synchronized via operational transformation.

temporal_info.transition_to_sharedb: ShareJS is now ShareDB, with discussions from 2015/2016.

references.name: ShareJS Client Connection Code (for query documentation)
ShareJS REST API (Moving to own repo)
